landing.title: Predict who's leaving — and how to keep them.
landing.subtitle: Soral scores each worker for turnover risk, explains why, and hands supervisors the play to retain them — a week before they walk.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need to act on attrition
landing.featuresSubtitle: From signal to intervention, in one workspace.
landing.f1Desc: Every worker as a dot, ranked by 30-day turnover risk. Hot spots stand out at a glance.
landing.f2Desc: SHAP contributions show why the model flags each person — no black box.
landing.f3Desc: A retention play generated for the manager, ready to assign to the supervisor.
landing.f4Desc: Fairness by group, proxy-signal alerts, and an auditable record of every decision.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Turn turnover from a surprise into a plan.
landing.howSubtitle: From raw HR data to a retention play — in three steps.
landing.step1Desc: Import headcount, attendance and HR signals via CSV, SFTP or API.
landing.step2Desc: The model ranks turnover risk per worker and shows the why with SHAP.
landing.step3Desc: Supervisors get a recommendation and assign it to act this week.
landing.explainTitle: No black box. Every score, explained.
landing.explainText: Soral shows the SHAP contribution of each factor and a six-dimension risk profile, so a supervisor knows exactly why a worker is flagged — and what to act on.
landing.simTitle: Move the levers. Watch risk drop.
landing.simText: Each lever is a retention play: raise it to apply it harder and watch high risk and cost drop. Just a demo — in Soral, each lever's effect is derived from every worker's real SHAP.
landing.previewBriefing: 6 high-risk this week — the focus is line L3 (22% turnover). Assign retention plans.
landing.previewLiveHint: This view is available in Soral. Open it to see it with your data.
landing.previewPlay2: Audit supervision style on the line.
landing.previewPlay3: Validate and adjust the transport route.
landing.baTitle: From surprise resignation to a plan in time
landing.baSubtitle: Same week, two realities. The difference is seeing the exit coming.
landing.baBefore1: The resignation lands on Monday, no warning.
landing.baBefore2: The line runs short by midweek.
landing.baBefore3: Recruiting, training and the ramp-up cost thousands per departure.
landing.baBefore4: You find out once the worker is already gone.
landing.baAfter2: A 1:1 retention plan in the supervisor's hands.
landing.baAfter3: A timely conversation — the worker stays.
landing.baAfter4: You know the real cost of each departure — and you prevent it.

model.title: Model & transparency
model.subtitle: How the turnover score is built, how accurate it is, and the guardrails around it.
model.calibSub: Predicted vs. observed turnover — the closer to the diagonal, the better calibrated.
model.howBody: Each worker gets a 0–100 probability of leaving in the next 30 days, combined from six dimensions of signal. SHAP shows exactly which factors drive each score — there is no black box.
model.confBody: Confidence is lower near the 80% decision boundary and higher at the extremes. Treat a low-confidence score as a prompt to look closer — not to act blindly.
model.gov1: No adverse action: a score must never justify discipline or termination — only retention support.
model.gov2: No protected attributes are used as features; bias is monitored across shifts, lines and tenure.
model.gov3: Every score is explainable (SHAP) and every assigned action is audit-logged.
model.gov4: Human in the loop: supervisors decide and act — the model only prioritizes.
model.validationNote: Validation cohort · n={n} · Brier {brier} · {lift}× lift over a {base} base rate · operating point at p≥{thr}. Synthetic until your data connects.
model.decompSub: A night-shift L3 operator. SHAP contributions sum exactly to the score.
model.signalsSub: Each score combines {n} signals across six families — every one tied to a connected source.

hiring.title: Pre-hire risk & cost
hiring.subtitle: Predict who will stay, and what a short hire would cost — before the offer.
hiring.govNote: Decision support, not automated rejection. Soral uses only job-related signals — never protected attributes — and a recruiter makes every call.
hiring.pipelineSub: Survival, expected tenure and early-exit cost — sorted by cost at risk.
hiring.calcHint: A short hire (exit under 90 days) costs about {cost} in recruiting, training and lost output.
hiring.calcFoot: Raising 90-day survival by even a few points compounds fast at maquila hiring volumes.
hiring.recapError: Could not generate the recap. Try again.
hiring.sourceRules: Rule-based recap · add an LLM key for richer recaps
hiring.recapNote: This recap is decision support only. It must not be the sole basis for a hiring decision, and it never uses protected attributes.

voice.title: Employee voice
voice.subtitle: AI listening across surveys, exit interviews and HR tickets — themes, sentiment and early warnings by line.
voice.sourceRules: Rule-based digest · add an LLM key for a written read
voice.themesSub: What workers are talking about, by sentiment and momentum.
voice.verbatimsSub: Anonymized quotes in their original language.
voice.alertMsg: {theme} on line {line} is sliding — act before it converts to exits.

floor.title: Floor
floor.actNowSub: Workers who need a play today.
floor.noAlerts: No high-risk workers on this line.
floor.myActionsSub: Advance each play and log the outcome.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.subtitle: Predicting who leaves is half the job. This randomized pilot proves that acting actually reduces turnover.
evidence.verdictSentence: Treated workers stay at {t}% vs {c}% for control — a gap that chance does not explain.
evidence.roiNote: At a {cost} replacement cost per early exit.
evidence.trendTitle: The effect becomes conclusive over time
evidence.trendSub: Cumulative uplift and its 95% CI by pilot week.
evidence.byLineSub: The effect is not uniform: some lines are not yet conclusive (CI crosses zero).
evidence.flywheelBody: Every outcome a supervisor logs (retained / left) is a label. As they accumulate, the model retrains and gains accuracy: the product improves with use.
evidence.methodBody: Randomized pilot: random assignment removes selection bias, so the gap between arms is due to the assigned play, not to who was already more fragile. The cohort is synthetic but statistically faithful: we inject a real per-worker effect and the engine recovers it (95% CI). On the real pilot it is replaced by the client holdout, without touching the contract or the UI.

governance.title: Governance & fairness
governance.subtitle: How the model behaves across groups, which signals could proxy a protected attribute, and an auditable record of every decision.
governance.fairnessSub: Risk rate the model assigns to each group. The impact ratio (4/5ths rule) flags gaps worth reviewing.
governance.proxySub: Model drivers that could correlate with a protected attribute. Not auto-removed — they're watched and the rationale documented.
governance.note_age: Watch that it doesn't penalize age: tenure must be read with line context, not as an individual factor.
governance.note_location: May reflect home location or socioeconomic level via the transport route. Check it doesn't concentrate risk in certain areas.
governance.note_finance: May reflect financial vulnerability. Use to support (advance, bonus), never as grounds for exit.
governance.note_family: Overtime refusal can correlate with caregiving load (a gender proxy). Treat as a load signal, not a commitment one.
governance.note_none: No known correlation with a protected attribute; job-related signal.
governance.logSub: Every assigned plan is traced: who, for whom, why, and with what outcome. The audit base of the intervention loop.
governance.calSub: Flagging evenly isn't enough — the model must be right evenly. Compares predicted risk with observed attrition; a large gap means it over- or under-estimates that group.
governance.method: These are population-level statistics (not the weekly worklist). The impact ratio follows the 4/5ths rule: below 0.80 warrants review. Calibration compares predicted risk with observed attrition per group.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: How Soral works, from signal to action — and how it stays explainable, proven, and governed.
docs.ctaTitle: Ready to see it in action?
docs.sec1Body: Soral predicts each worker's attrition 30 days ahead, explains why, and hands the supervisor the play to retain them. Built for maquiladoras, where turnover is high and expensive.
docs.sec2Body: A five-step loop: connect your HR data, the model scores and explains the risk, the supervisor gets a recommendation and acts, the outcome is measured, and those labels retrain the model. Every loop makes it sharper.
docs.sec3Body: A survival model ranks 30-day risk across six dimensions: punctuality, attendance, tenure, productivity, overtime, and supervisor. Every score ships with its SHAP breakdown — never a black box, always the why.
docs.sec4Body: The action plan turns risk into a weekly worklist, and the what-if simulator shows each intervention's effect before you apply it. A randomized pilot measures the real causal effect (ATE, 95% CI, ROI): proof that acting cuts turnover.
docs.sec5Body: Soral measures how the model treats each group (line, shift, tenure) with the 4/5ths rule, flags signals that could proxy a protected attribute, and keeps an auditable record of every decision. What an ethics committee or a union expects.
docs.sec6Body: It connects to HRIS, payroll, time-clock and biometrics (SuccessFactors, ADP, UKG, ZKTeco…). RBAC from day one, a three-language interface, and sensitive data never leaves the server — the client/server boundary is the security layer.

cases.title: Use cases
cases.subtitle: How plant teams use Soral to curb turnover, hire better, and prove ROI.
cases.c1Body: A line running 22% 90-day turnover shows up as a red hotspot on the map. The supervisor sees who's at risk and why, and assigns the week's retention play — before they quit.
cases.c2Body: Before hiring, Soral estimates how long a candidate would stay and what it'd cost if they leave early. It doesn't decide for you — it gives you the evidence to decide better.
cases.c3Body: Surveys, exit interviews and tickets become themes and sentiment by line. Early alerts flag where discontent is rising before it turns into exits.
cases.c4Body: A randomized experiment (treated vs control) measures the causal effect of interventions with real statistics. You turn 'we think it works' into a business case with numbers.
cases.c5Body: Fairness by group, proxy signals and a record of every decision, ready for Legal, the ethics committee or the union. AI that affects people, with accountability.

costModel.title: Turnover cost
costModel.subtitle: Calculate the real cost of each departure with your own numbers. Until you configure it, Soral uses a reference estimate and labels it as such across the app.
costModel.estimateNote: Reference estimate — configure it with your plant’s numbers.
costModel.appliesNote: Applies to the "Cost at risk" KPI, the simulator and ROI.
